data.title: SoFi Stock Is Up 62% — Here's What Could Push It Higher (or Bring It Back Down)
data.description:
SoFi Technologies (SOFI) has become one of the most closely watched fintech stocks after rising nearly 62% during the past year. This rally was largely fueled by the firm's impressive financial results. However, investors will now be trying to figure out what could drive the stock going forward. As a result, the following sections look at the key forces that could push SoFi higher, as well as the risks that could hold it back.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Catalysts that May Push the Stock Higher

One reason why investors may remain optimistic is SoFi's recently launched SoFiUSD, a U.S. dollar-backed stablecoin that could unlock new revenue and fintech infrastructure opportunities by offering faster payment settlement. Additionally, CEO Anthony Noto recently highlighted that President Trump's proposed credit card interest rate cap of 10% could be a potential tailwind. More specifically, he suggested that if traditional lenders pull back due to the rate cap, SoFi's personal loans business could benefit from increased demand

Another potential catalyst is SoFi's upcoming earnings report at the end of January. If the company delivers better-than-expected results or raises guidance for 2026, it could boost investor sentiment and lead to upward revisions to earnings estimates. This would help the stock maintain its premium valuation multiple, which is currently supported by SoFi's strong annual recurring revenue and profitability, especially when considering that its peers still struggle to turn a profit.

Moreover, membership and product growth remain bright spots, with Wall Street praising the company's ability to attract and retain customers. It also helps that SoFi's relatively young customer base and digital-first model position it well with younger consumers who prefer online banking and financial products.

Risks to Watch Out For

That said, there are risks that could put pressure on SoFi stock. One near-term concern is investor worry over share dilution after SoFi raised about $1.5 billion through a stock offering late last year. The issue is that more shares reduce per-share earnings, which could make it more difficult for SoFi to beat upcoming estimates. Just like an earnings beat can cause upward earnings revisions, a miss can lead to downward revisions, making it harder to justify its premium valuation.

Separately, overall market forces also matter, since the financial sector is cyclical. In fact, if credit conditions tighten or consumer borrowing slows down, SoFi's results could be negatively impacted. And while regulatory ideas like interest rate caps might benefit SoFi's loan growth, there's no guarantee that such measures will actually become law.

Is SoFi Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on SOFI stock based on five Buys, six Holds, and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average SOFI price target of $28.31 per share implies 7.1% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Citigroup's (C) Record Earnings Overshadowed by Credit Card Exposure
data.description:
America's largest bank, Citigroup (C), reported record quarterly earnings yesterday, exceeding analyst expectations on solid performance across most business segments, with Markets revenue the only segment that failed to outpace expense growth. While the headline results were affected by the divestment of Citi's Russia business, I believe the primary driver of the market's negative reaction was the bank's exposure to credit card loans—an area facing increased scrutiny amid proposals to cap credit card interest rates at 10%.

data.title: U.S.-Taiwan Secure Trade Deal with $500 Billion AI and Chip Investment
data.description:
The U.S. and Taiwan have agreed to a trade deal that will see Taiwanese technology companies invest at least $250 billion into the U.S. for semiconductor, energy, and artificial intelligence operations. Furthermore, Taiwan's government has agreed to provide a minimum of $250 billion in credit guarantees to Taiwanese enterprises for investing in the U.S. semiconductor supply chain.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In exchange, the U.S. will lower the self-governing island's tariff rate to 15% from 20% and exempt its generic pharmaceuticals, the ingredients used in those drugs, aircraft components, and some natural resources from tariffs.

TSM Eyes Arizona Expansion Under U.S.-Taiwan Agreement

In addition, Taiwanese companies that contribute to new U.S. semiconductor capacity "may import up to 2.5 times that planned capacity without paying Section 232 duties during the approved construction period." Taiwanese companies that complete new chip projects in the U.S. will be able to import 1.5 times the capacity of finished facilities.

Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, who led negotiations on the U.S. side, told CNBC that Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) recently purchased land in Arizona and is considering expanding its presence there as part of the deal.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: NVDA vs. AMD vs. AVGO: RBC's Top Analyst Picks the Best Chip Stock
data.description:
RBC Capital analyst Srini Pajjuri initiated coverage of the semiconductor industry, with specific focus on Nvidia (NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and Broadcom (AVGO). He gave NVDA a "Buy" rating, while giving a "Hold" rating to AMD and AVGO shares.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Pajjuri sees "limited threat" to Nvidia's full-stack AI dominance, reinforcing his bullish stance on the stock. In contrast, he does not see many immediate catalysts for AMD until MI450 ramps for OpenAI in Q4. For Broadcom, he cited its high valuation relative to leader Nvidia as a concerning factor.

Pajjuri is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #227 out of 12,040 analysts tracked. He has a 73% success rate and an impressive average return per rating of 36.60%.

NVDA Stock: A Leader in Full-Stack AI

Pajjuri assigned a price target of $240 on NVDA, implying 31.1% upside potential from current levels. He argued that Nvidia maintains a dominant position in full-stack AI, facing limited incremental threat from recent ASIC advancements and competitors such as AMD. He does not anticipate any sharp, widespread slowdown in hyperscaler capital expenditure, but argues that any deceleration would be gradual and already largely reflected in current valuations.

Additionally, he has higher conviction in Nvidia's order book relative to its peer group, supporting the thesis that Nvidia's revenue outlook remains robust despite evolving competitive dynamics.

AMD Stock: No Immediate Catalysts

Pajjuri assigned a $230 price target to AMD, signaling only a modest 2.9% upside potential from current levels. He views the OpenAI partnership as a pivotal validation of AMD's viability as a GPU supplier for hyperscale initiatives, signaling credible competitive positioning against incumbents like Nvidia.

However, despite management's strong confidence in achieving an initial 1GW production ramp, Pajjuri believes that the stock price already factors in that growth, limiting near-term upside potential. His analysis highlights a scarcity of immediate catalysts, with meaningful momentum expected only after the MI450 chips ramp up for OpenAI in Q4. Overall, he has a cautious view due to risks in pulling it off.

AVGO Stock: High Valuation Relative to Peers

Pajjuri set a $370 price target on AVGO, implying 8.9% upside potential. He acknowledged robust near-term momentum in Broadcom's TPU (Tensor Processing Unit or custom accelerators) segment, driven by hyperscaler adoption. However, Pajjuri flags uncertainties surrounding the durability of the Anthropic production ramp, alongside opaque timing and scale for the prospective OpenAI engagement, tempering long-term growth visibility.

Moreover, he highlighted that Broadcom's valuation trades at a 25% premium to Nvidia even following recent market corrections, suggesting limited relative appeal amid comparable AI exposure.

NVDA, AMD, AVGO: Which Is the Best Chip Stock?

The TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool shows that analysts have assigned a "Strong Buy" consensus rating on three stocks, with NVDA offering the highest upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Why Taiwan Semi's CEO Expects a 'Multiyear AI Megatrend' Following Blowout Quarter
data.description:
C.C. Wei, the CEO of chipmaker Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM), recently pushed back against claims that the artificial intelligence boom is a bubble. More specifically, he said that he spent the last three to four months talking directly with his customers and their customers to see if AI demand is real. And based on those conversations, Wei said that the company's "conviction in the multiyear AI megatrend remains strong," as companies are already seeing AI improve their businesses.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As a result, TSM is increasing its capital spending to about $54 billion in 2026 from roughly $41 billion in 2025. At the same time, the company said that its AI-related revenue is expected to grow at a high-50% yearly rate through 2029. These comments, along with strong fourth-quarter results that beat expectations, helped lift chip stocks across the market. Meanwhile, big tech companies that rely heavily on chips, such as Amazon (AMZN) and Meta Platforms (META), also edged higher.

Moreover, analysts added to that optimism by pointing out what TSMC's outlook means for the rest of the industry. For instance, Wedbush analyst Matthew Bryson said that the company's results and guidance suggest "strong continued orders" for chip designers like Nvidia (NVDA) and Broadcom (AVGO). This matters because TSMC's largest customers include Apple (AAPL), Nvidia, AMD (AMD), Broadcom, Qualcomm (QCOM), and Amazon, with Nvidia alone accounting for about 13% of its revenue.

Is TSM Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on TSM stock based on eight Buys, one Hold, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSM price target of $326.33 per share implies 4.5% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: TSM Stock Wins a New Street-High Price Target After Blockbuster Results
data.description:
Needham's top analyst Charles Shi raised his price target on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) after its blockbuster fourth quarter results and above-consensus guidance. Shi kept his Buy rating and lifted the price target from $360 to a new Street-high of $410, implying 25.3% upside potential from current levels. TSM stock rallied to a new 52-week high of $351.20 following the results today.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The contract chip manufacturer reported a 35% jump in Q4 profit, surpassing estimates, driven by robust demand for AI chips. For Q4FY25, TSM posted NT$505.74 billion ($16 billion) in net income and diluted EPS of NT$19.50 ($3.14 per ADR). Meanwhile, revenue increased 25.5% year-over-year to NT$1.046 trillion ($33.73 billion), beating the expected NT$1.034 trillion.

Shi is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #313 out of 12,040 analysts tracked. He has a 58% success rate and an average return per rating of 27.50%.

Analyst Raises Estimates on Bullish Outlook

Shi highlighted the following key upgrades in TSMC's guidance.

  • 2026 Revenue Growth: Projected near 30%, surpassing the Street's 25% estimate and his own prior 25% model.
  • 2026 CapEx: Expected at $52-$56 billion, above his prior $50 billion forecast.
  • AI Revenue CAGR (through 2029): Raised to mid-to-high 50s%, from previous mid-40s%.
  • Long-Term Revenue CAGR: Now "approaching" 25%, up from 20%.
  • Through-Cycle Gross Margin: Elevated to "56% and higher," from "53% and higher."

Shi noted that TSMC's updated outlook significantly outpaces prior Street consensus and his own model. In turn, he revised forecasts upward, projecting TSMC's total 2027 revenue to approach $200 billion, up from the previous $182 billion, and AI revenue to hit $120 billion (previously $90 billion). This justifies the new $410 price target amid robust AI-driven momentum.

Is TSM a Good Stock to Buy?

Analysts remain highly optimistic about Taiwan Semiconductor's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, TSMC has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on seven Buys and one Hold rating. The average Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing price target of $320.61 implies nearly 2% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSM shares have gained over 58%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Cathie Wood Hits the Sell Button on Tesla Stock (TSLA) — Should Investors Be Concerned?
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) shares are up about 41% over the past six months, driven by renewed optimism around AI and self-driving. Still, the stock fell about 2% on Wednesday after Tesla said it will stop selling its Full Self-Driving package after February 14. Adding to the caution, Cathie Wood's ARK Invest sold 86,136 shares, worth roughly $38.5 million.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meanwhile, Wall Street remains divided. While some analysts back Tesla's long-term AI and autonomy plans, others warn that much of that optimism is already priced in, with margins under pressure. As a result, the overall view on TSLA remains cautious, suggesting investors may want to balance long-term promise with near-term risks.

Analysts' Opinions on TSLA Stock 

Recently, UBS analyst Joseph Spak kept a Sell rating on Tesla stock but increased the price target to $307 from $247, saying the stock already reflects most of the hype around robotaxis and the Optimus robot. He noted that Tesla shares have risen even as EV sales slow and profit forecasts fall, which suggests investors are putting more value on future AI projects than on the core car business.

Spak said these AI projects could be big over time, but they are still far off, especially Optimus. While 2026 may bring updates like wider robotaxi use, new software, and product launches, he believes many of these positives are already priced into the stock. As a result, valuation remains the key risk, with limited upside from current levels.

Meanwhile, New Street Research named Tesla a 'Top Pick,' saying the company is well placed to lead the next stage of self-driving transport. The firm kept a Buy rating on the stock and its Street-high $600 price target.

New Street said the robotaxi market could reach a key turning point in 2026, as more companies move from testing to real-world use. With players like Waymo and Tesla expanding commercial services, the firm believes Tesla stands out from its peers and is best positioned to take the lead as the market opens up.

Is Tesla a Buy, Sell, or Hold? 

Turning to Wall Street, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and eight Sells assigned in the last three months. At $394.12, the average Tesla price target implies a 10.26% downside risk.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Climb on U.S.-Taiwan Trade Deal; Easing Jobless Claims
data.description:
The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) finished Thursday in positive territory, although both ETFs fell from their intraday highs during the last two hours of the trading session.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In a sign of a resilient labor market, initial jobless claims for the week ended January 9 decreased by 9,000 to 198,000, below the consensus estimate of 215,000. Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, fell by 19,000 to 1.884 million and were also below the expectation of 1.897 million. Initial claims serve as a gauge of layoffs, while continuing claims track the number of people receiving unemployment benefits.

Meanwhile, the U.S. and Taiwan have agreed to a trade deal that will see the self-governing island's tech firms commit to investing at least $250 billion into semiconductor, AI, and energy operations in the U.S. In addition, the Taiwanese government will provide a minimum of $250 billion in credit guarantees to Taiwanese companies for investing in the U.S. semiconductor ecosystem. In return, the U.S. will drop Taiwan's tariff rate to 15% from 20% and exempt generic pharmaceuticals, the ingredients used in those drugs, aircraft components, and some natural resources from duties.

Elsewhere, two Fed officials warned of steep consequences if the Fed loses its independence amid a Justice Department probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell over the central bank's $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters. "Anything that's infringing or attacking the independence of the central bank is a mess," Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee told CNBC's "Squawk Box." "You're going to get inflation come roaring back if you try to take away the independence of the central bank."

Furthermore, Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr framed the investigation as an "assault on the independence of the Fed." Barr stressed that the Fed's monetary policy decisions take into account inflation and the labor market while casting aside political biases.

Finally, President Trump is considering invoking the Insurrection Act to address mass protests against Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents in Minnesota. The federal law allows the president to deploy the U.S. military domestically to help suppress unrest, insurrection, or rebellion. Earlier this week, Minnesota Governor Tim Walz accused the Trump administration of "organized brutality" after an ICE agent fatally shot 37-year-old Minneapolis resident Renee Good.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.26% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 0.32%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Duolingo Stock (DUOL) Offers Volatility-Fueled Buying Opportunity
data.description:
Duolingo's (DUOL) press release issued on Monday landed poorly and only extended the stock's already brutal sell-off. Investors seem to have zeroed in on the CFO transition and management's commitment to continue pouring money into product development rather than focusing on juicing near-term margins.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Yet the document itself doesn't read like a profit warning. I translated it more like management saying, "we're still compounding, we're just not bending our strategy to short-term market noise." With momentum intact and the valuation now at rather depressed levels, I remain bullish and happily selling puts to capitalize on the ongoing volatility.
data.author: Nikolaos Sismanis

data.title: NVDA, GOOGL, META: AI Spending Forecast to Hit $2.53 Trillion This Year
data.description:
Spending on artificial intelligence (AI) shows no signs of slowing down anytime soon.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to business and technology insights firm Gartner (IT), global AI spending will reach $2.53 trillion in 2026 and climb to $3.33 trillion in 2027 as companies continue to pour money into the revolutionary technology that promises to transform life as we know it.

In a new outlook, Gartner says that the bulk of the spending over the next two years will be focused on AI infrastructure, with hyperscalers such as Google parent Alphabet (GOOGL), Meta Platforms (META), and Amazon (AMZN) expected to invest $1.36 trillion to build the backbone of AI in 2026 and another $1.75 trillion in 2027.

Chipmakers Will Spend Heavily, Too

Chipmakers such as Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) are also expected to continue spending heavily to create ever more powerful processors capable of running AI workloads and the data centers built by the hyperscalers.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has forecast that the chipmaker will sell $500 billion worth of AI chips and semiconductors this year. AMD CEO Lisa Su has said that the AI data center market alone is likely to be worth $1 trillion to chipmakers by 2030.

Gartner adds that heavy spending is likely to continue in the development of AI software, models, and data science over the next few years.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Nvidia's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 41 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 39 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the past three months. The average NVDA price target of $262.75 implies 40.57% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole
